text: ‘The Latin version here seems to imply that the posts themselves were made of bronze. But there is no such indication in 27.10, and verse 17 below tells us that the posts were plated with silver, and therefore probably made of wood. It seems possible therefore that the Latin text is only an awkward rendering of the Hebrew, which clearly says that thesockets were of bronze.

The Hebrew text has ‘seventy talents and two thousand four hundred sicles’, a much more likely figure. It is probable that the talent represents about half a hundredweight, and the sicle about half an ounce; but the figures, according to others, should be considerably lower.
